title: Dasurus luteus
uses: Dasurus luteus has been found to have antimalarial properties, with its extracts showing potential in treating the disease. In traditional medicine, the plant is also used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Research has shown that the plant contains bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The leaves and roots of Dasurus luteus are often used in folk remedies to alleviate symptoms of malaria and other diseases. Further studies are needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of this plant and its potential applications in modern medicine.
taxonomy: Dasurus luteus is classified within the family Convolvulaceae, which is a family of flowering plants. This family is further grouped into the order Solanales. The order Solanales includes other plant families such as Solanaceae and Petunia. Within the Convolvulaceae family, Dasurus luteus belongs to the tribe Ipomoeae. The specific classification of Dasurus luteus is still a topic of ongoing research in the field of botany.

properties: Dasurus luteus, also known as yellow garden spider, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. The venom from this spider has been researched for its possible applications in pain management and as a treatment for certain types of cancer. constituents: Dasurus luteus contains active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids present in Dasurus luteus are also known to have potential anti-cancer and cardiovascular-protective effects. Furthermore, the plant's terpenoids have been found to have antibacterial and antifungal properties. The exact composition of active constituents in Dasurus luteus may vary depending on the plant's growing conditions and geographic location.
parts: Dasurus luteus, also known as the golden chanterelle, is a type of fungus. Dasurus luteus has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with the medicinal parts being the fruit body, specifically the caps and stems. The fruit body contains compounds that have antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiviral properties, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions. The medicinal parts of Dasurus luteus have been used to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
